page: Microsoft Azure DS12_v2 is a virtual machine instance in the DSv2 family powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 50 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA. The instance is configured with 28.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 57 GB of local storage. No GPU resources are bundled with this server.

Benchmark results indicate that the DS12_v2 exhibits lower-tier performance across most compute and memory metrics. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% or bottom 10% categories. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks similarly rank in the bottom 25%, with read-only large block bandwidth measured at 31.02 GB/sec. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in LLM inference tasks using a small 135M parameter model, achieving 582.75 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 167.21 tokens/sec for text generation. Performance drops significantly on larger models, with 7B model prompt processing falling into the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DS12_v2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7:1, which provides a relatively high resource density of RAM for workloads that are not heavily CPU-bound. The tradeoff is the older Intel Xeon architecture, which limits raw computational throughput and memory bandwidth compared to newer hardware generations. This instance is suitable for memory-intensive workloads with moderate CPU demands, such as small-scale database operations, caching layers, basic web serving, and lightweight text or language model processing.
description: Microsoft Azure DS12_v2 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance within the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor at 2.3 GHz, 28.0 GB of RAM (7.0 GB per core), and 57 GB of local storage. The instance does not include a GPU. Performance benchmarks place the DS12_v2 in the lower tier for general compute, memory bandwidth, and compression tasks, with most results falling into the bottom 25% or bottom 10%. However, it maintains average performance for small-scale LLM inference (135M model). Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7:1, the DS12_v2 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, CPU-light workloads. It is best suited for database operations, caching, and moderate web serving.
